ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Abram bena: Ʋu le wò anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ kple fofowò ƒe me, eye nayi anyigba, si mafia wò la dzi!
translated: And Jehovah said to Abram: “I will make you a great nation, and I will bless you and make your name great; and you will be a blessing.

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Esia megbe la Abraham ɖi srɔ̃a Sara ɖe kpeto, si le Maxpela-gblea me la me le Mamre kasa; esia enye Hebron le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.
translated: Abraham and Sarah lived in Mamre, near Hebron, in Canaan.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: Eye Yosef ƒe aƒetɔ lée de gaxɔ, si me fia ƒe gatɔwo nɔna la me, eye wònɔ afima le gaxɔa me.
translated: Joseph was a good man, and he was kind to everyone. He was also a very honest man.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Tete amea ɖe si va gblɔe na Hebritɔ Abram, si le Amoritɔ Mamre, Eskol kple Aner nɔvi ƒe ali gãwo te la. Ame siawoe bia nu kple Abram.
translated: And it came to pass that Abraham, the Hebrew, dwelt by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and Aner; these three had a covenant with Abraham.

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Eɖo ŋu ne bena: Betuel, Milka vi, si wòdzi na Naxor la vinyɔnu menye.
translated: A man named Betuel, Milka's son, lived in Naxor, a city in Mesopotamia.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Eye Mawu wɔ ame ɖe ye ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme, Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wòwɔe ɖo; ŋutsu kple nyɔnu wòwɔ.
translated: God created the world and everything in it. God's creation is good; the world and everything in it are good.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ke mabia nu kpli wò bena, nayi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃wò hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋu.
translated: When a child is born, it is the mother who knows best how to care for it. She knows its needs, its cries, and its desires.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ismael netsi agbe na wò ko! Mawu ɖo Isak ŋugbe na Abraham.
translated: And Abraham said to God: "But Ishmael will also be your heir!" God said to Abraham: "No, Isaac will be your heir."

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.
translated: Tete Lea was a good man who served God faithfully. He was a strong and brave warrior, and he was known for his wisdom and justice. His name was Zebulon.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Meɖe kuku, ɖem tso nɔvinye si me, tso Esau si me; elabena mele evɔ̃m be, ava dze dzinye, eye wòaɖe zi dadawo kple viwo dome.
translated: I am a man, and my name is Jacob, the same as my brother Esau; but my mother loved me more than him, and she gave me the best food and clothing.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Azɔ ka atam le Mawu ƒe ŋku me le afisia be, nagable nye ŋutɔ, vinyewo kple nye dzidzimeviwo o! Abe alesi meve nu wòe ene la, nenema ke wò hã nawɔ nam kple anyigba, si dzi nènye amedzro le la!
translated: This is a prayer to God, asking for his blessings on the speaker's family, friends, and loved ones. The speaker expresses gratitude for the good things in their life and asks for continued protection and guidance.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Eye Yakob ɖe lãviawo ɖo Laban ƒe lã, siwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la kasa. Ale wòɖo lãha tɔxe na eɖokui, eye mena wotɔtɔ kple Laban towo o.
translated: Jacob went to Laban's land, and he worked for him for seven years, and they agreed on wages. After that, he gave him his daughter Rachel as a wife, and he worked for him for another seven years.

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: Ɖe mele mia bum amedzrowoe oa? Elabena etsɔ mí dzra, eye wòle ga, si wòxɔ ɖe mía ta la ɖum vaseɖe mi.
translated: What do you want from me? I have told you the truth, and I have nothing to hide, so why are you still asking me?

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Tete wotsɔ nɔvia wo kpe ɖe eɖokui ŋu ti eyome ŋkeke adre sɔŋ, eye wòva tui le Gilead-towo dzi.
translated: The Lord is my shepherd, I shall not want. He makes me lie down in green pastures; he leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul; he leads me in paths of righteousness for his name's sake.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: Le ɣleti evelia ƒe ŋkeke blave vɔ adrelia dzi anyigba dzi ƒu keŋkeŋ. Noa me nu na Mawu.
translated: The world is full of troubles, but we should always thank God.

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Ɖe nane sesẽ na Yehowa? Le ƒe, si gbɔna ƒe woleawoɣi la, magato ava gbɔwò, eye ŋutsuvi anɔ Sara si.
translated: What is Jehovah's name? Yes, it is a wonderful name, and it is a name that brings comfort, and it is a name that is precious to Sarah.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Emegbe la egadzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Sela. Enɔ Kesib hafi dzii.
translated: The woman went to the farm, and she found Sela there. He was working on the farm.

ref: “I'll name the boy Joseph, and I'll pray that the Lord will give me another son.” Jacob and Laban
text: Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Yosef; elabena egblɔ be: Yehowa agatsɔ ŋutsuvi bubu akpee nam. Yakob ƒe kesinɔnuwo.
translated: Joseph was a faithful servant of Jehovah, a righteous man. He was a descendant of Jacob.

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: Gbemagbe ke wòɖe gbɔ̃tsu, siwo me fli to, kple esiwo tso kpɔ, kple gbɔ̃nɔ, siwo katã le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ — lã sia lã, si me ɣi to — kpakple alẽvi, siwo le yibɔ la, ɖe vovo, eye wòde wo asi na viawo.
translated: When a person is sick, they should be taken care of, given medicine, and fed, and their bed should be made comfortable, and they should be given water to drink — little by little, as they can take it — and they should be kept warm, and their surroundings should be clean, and they should be comforted.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Tete wona wein wo fofo le zã ma me wono, eye tsitsitɔ yi ɖamlɔ fofoa gbɔ; ke eya menya naneke le eƒe egbɔ mɔmlɔ kple fɔfɔdzo ŋuti o.
translated: That person is very good, and he is helping us a lot. He is the one who brought us peace and development.
